decision: At fixed 12.5M token-only architecture, four trajectories per clean sequence, detached action prior, exact terminal token-edit teacher, and 512 clean sequences, does faithful GAR supervision—MSE regression weight 0.25 plus same-state pairwise margin ranking—create a discriminative deployment V(s,a), and does its benefit increase with target-independent counterfactual breadth K in {1,4,8}? K=1 regression-only anchors the changed regression scale, K=8 regression-only isolates breadth without ranking, and a K=8 learning-rate cross-check tests whether the new gradient scale merely changes optimization. Continue to deeper MPC only if pairwise/top-1 accuracy and posterior discrimination improve without damaging action-conditioned transition controls.

jobs.purpose: Matched K=1 MSE-0.25 regression-only control for the ranked GAR objective.
K=1 expert-versus-counterfactual pairwise GAR control at MSE weight 0.25.
Four-counterfactual same-state GAR ranking cell measuring the first breadth increase.
Eight-counterfactual regression-only control separating extra labels from pairwise ordering.
Eight-counterfactual ranked GAR cell testing whether wider same-state ordering changes useful geometry.
Lower-learning-rate cross-check for the widest ranked GAR cell after the objective changes gradient magnitude.
